title: Fern and Wren and the Wrong Potion

pageTimings.text:
Fern's garden was her favourite place.
One morning, Wren arrived with a cauldron.
"I have a potion," said Wren.
"It will help your plants."

"Are you sure?" said Fern.
"Completely," said Wren.
She had practised many times.
Mostly.

Wren stirred the potion.
It was meant to be green.
It was more purple.
Wren did not notice this.

Wren poured the potion on the plants.
The plants stood very straight.
Then they leaned.
Then one of them took a step.

Then all the plants began to walk.
They were very polite about it.
They walked around Fern.
They walked around Wren.
They went for a stroll.

"Oh," said Wren.
"Oh no," said Fern.
"The plants are walking."
Wren checked her book.

The plants walked into the Hollow Oak.
Some walked toward the Crystal Stream.
A very small plant walked very slowly toward Burrow's tunnel.
They were not in a hurry.
They were just walking.

Wren found the problem.
She had used the wrong book.
"This is the motion recipe," she said.
"Not the growing recipe."
She showed Fern.
They were both quiet for a moment.

Fern went to the middle of the clearing.
She closed her eyes.
She sang.
Very softly.
The same song from long ago.

The plants slowed.
The far ones turned.
They walked back.
Slowly.
Like sleepy creatures going home.

One by one they found their places.
Each plant settled.
The smallest sat down last.
It made a small sound.
It seemed happy.

Everything was back.
"I'm sorry," said Wren.
"Did you learn something?" asked Fern.
"Yes," said Wren. "I need more books."
"Fewer," said Fern.
"Fewer books."
